_schema.purpose: Advisory-allowlist registry for validate_consistency.sh checks. Each entry suppresses ONE known false positive so the validator can report clean state when the pool is actually clean. Every entry MUST include a reason — periodic reviewers should be able to audit at a glance whether the suppression is still load-bearing.
_schema.review_cadence: Audit on every /pipeline-audit run. If a suppression's reason no longer applies (regex tightened, doc rewritten, unit convention standardised), delete the entry and let the validator surface the case again.

fields.key: for check_param_defaults: param name (e.g. 's38_immobile'). For check_multi_section_consistency: variable name (e.g. 'vm_land')
fields.reason: why this is a false positive (one sentence)
fields.added: ISO date the entry was added

allowlist.reason: Doc table column correctly shows default=1; the regex greedy-matches '100' from the description prose '(default: 100% immobile)' where 100% is the human-readable rendering of the 1.0 share. Not a real mismatch.
Unit-conversion false positive: doc says '200 kg N/ha', source value is 0.2 t N/ha. Same physical quantity, different unit. The checker does not perform unit normalization.
